classes.description: Small fast patrol and interception boats
Supply, survey, and logistics support vessels
State-affiliated ocean research and survey vessels conducting seabed mapping, sensor deployment, and oceanographic data collection with dual-use (civil-military fusion) capability
examples: Dong Fang Hong 3 (東方紅3號) - Ocean University of China
Tan Suo Er Hao (探索二號) - Deep-sea research
Zhong Shan Da Xue Hao (中山大學號) - Sun Yat-sen University
Xiang Yang Hong series (向陽紅系列) - Ministry of Natural Resources
Ke Xue Hao (科學號) - Chinese Academy of Sciences
Da Yang Hao (大洋號) - Deep ocean survey
Shi Yan series (實驗系列) - Experimental research
classes.notes: Per Reuters (2026-03-24): At least 42 research vessels tracked across Pacific, Indian, and Arctic oceans. 8 confirmed conducting seabed mapping, 10 more carrying mapping equipment. These vessels serve China's 'Transparent Ocean' (透明海洋) program and civil-military fusion strategy.

classes.description: Deployed underwater sensors, buoys, and subsea arrays for oceanographic monitoring with submarine detection capability
examples: Ninety East Ridge sensor network (Indian Ocean)
classes.notes: Hundreds deployed across Pacific and Indian oceans. Collect water temperature, salinity, current data. Capable of detecting submarine transits per USN ONI testimony (Rear Admiral Mike Brookes, March 2026).

changelog.changes: Added Class 5 (PRC Research Vessels) and Class 6 (Ocean Sensors/Buoys) based on Reuters investigation of China's undersea mapping campaign
Initial release with 5 CCG vessel classes
